ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT GABRIEL JACKSON'S DEMURRER TO ENTIRE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, HER DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL TORT IS OFF CALENDAR SINCE NO SUCH CAUSE OF ACTION IS ALLEGED AGAINST HER, HER DEMURRERS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND FRAUD ARE OVERRULED AND HER DEMURRERS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION AND NEGLIGENCE ARE SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, LIBERALLY CONSRUED, ALLEGES THAT MS. JACKSON PERSONALLY AND ON HER OWN BEHALF, SEPARATE FROM JACKSON & WALLACE, ENTERED INTO A CONTRACT AND BREACHED THAT CONTRACT, ALL OF WHICH STATES A CLAIM FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AGAINST HER. THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD, LIBERALLY CONSTRUED, ALLEGES ALL THE ELEMENTS OF PROMISSORY FRAUD (CACI 1902) AND COMES WITHIN CORPORATIONS CODE 16306(E). THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE AND CONVERSION ARE BASED ON ALLEGED FAILURES TO UNDERTAKE CERTAIN ACTIONS BUT THERE IS NO ALLEGATION THAT MS. JACKSON OWED A DUTY TO THE PLAINTIFF TO TAKE SUCH ACTIONS. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Daniel O'Shea, Esq., of JACKSON, JENKINS & RENSTROM, LLP (415-982-3600), appearing for defendant / moving party Peter Renstrom
Phillip S. Berry, Esq., of BERRY & BERRY (510-250-0200), appearing via CourtCall for plaintiff / opposing party Berry & Berry

RULING:
DEFENDANT PETER RENSTROM'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Peter Renstrom's demurrer to the entire second amended complaint is overruled, his demurrers to the causes of action for breach of contract and intentional tort are off calendar since no such causes of action are alleged against him, his demurrer to the cause of action for fraud is overruled and his demurrers to the causes of action for conversion and negligence are sustained with ten days leave to amend. The cause of action for fraud, liberally construed, alleges all the elements of promissory fraud (CACI 1902) and comes within Corporations Code 16306(e). The causes of action for negligence and conversion are based on alleged failures to undertake certain actions but there is no allegation that Mr. Renstrom owed a duty to the plaintiff to take such actions. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Gabriel Jackson's demurrer to entire second amended complaint is overruled, her demurrer to the cause of action for intentional tort is off calendar since no such cause of action is alleged against her, her demurrers to the causes of action for breach of contract and fraud are overruled and her demurrers to the causes of action for conversion and negligence are sustained with ten days leave to amend. The cause of action for breach of contract, liberally consrued, alleges that Ms. Jackson personally and on her own behalf, separate from Jackson & Wallace, entered into a contract and breached that contract, all of which states a claim for breach of contract against her. The causes of action for negligence and conversion are based on alleged failures to undertake certain actions but there is no allegation that Ms. Jackson owed a duty to the plaintiff to take such actions. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Daniel O'Shea, Esq., of JACKSON, JENKINS & RENSTROM, LLP (415-982-3600), appearing for defendant / moving party Gabriel Jackson

RULING:
DEFENDANT GABRIEL JACKSON'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT
No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Gabriel Jackson's demurrer to first amended complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The first amended complaint purports to allege causes of action for breach of contract and common counts, but no attachments on these purported causes of action were attached. The first purported cause of action for 'intentional tort' appears to be a claim for conversion (civil theft), but that is not stated explicitly. The third purported cause of action for breach of trust is not a cognizable claim. Nor is it clear whether a general unspecified claim for intentional tort is sought to be alleged. For guidance of the parties in the future, Ms. Jackson is subject to a suit where it is alleged that she personally committed misconduct, apart from any misconduct that may have been committed by others that are attributable to Jackson & Wallace. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
